title: Dryopteris obtusissima
taxonomy: Dryopteris obtusissima is a species of fern in the family Dryopteridaceae. This plant is classified as a member of the genus Dryopteris, which consists of approximately 200 species of wood ferns. Dryopteris obtusissima is native to Mexico and Central America. It belongs to the kingdom Plantae and the phylum Pteridophyta, which includes all vascular plants that produce spores. The species is further classified into the division Pteridophyta, class Polypodiopsida, and order Polypodiales.

The plant is traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive problems. intro: Dryopteris obtusissima, also known as the obtusely lanced-leaf buckler fern, is a species of fern that belongs to the Dryopteridaceae family. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to promote overall well-being and has been reported to possess various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, as well as potential anti-cancer and neuroprotective effects. The bioactive constituents of Dryopteris obtusissima include flavonoids, terpenoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, the plant is often prepared as a tea, tincture, or infused oil to support digestive health, reduce inflammation, and alleviate symptoms of anxiety and stress.
